_doc: Volume corpus eval cases — the UNSATURATED companion to eval_retrieval.cases.json. Where the cribsheet-only set has saturated recall (which is exactly why summary_index showed net-negative lift there, docs/retrieval-and-adoption.md §5/'Findings'), this set spans the diverse volume corpora imported for that reason (dotfiler, mcp-companion, svg-mcp, zdot, sharedserver). Each need targets a DISTINCTIVE section in one corpus and carries 3 phrasings: phrasing 1 is direct (near the note's own vocabulary), phrasings 2-3 are deliberately vocabulary-shifted (the querier's words, not the author's) — that query!=note gap is precisely what doc2query summary_index aliases (§3 items 2-3) are meant to close, and what tier-2 LLM keywords (§3.1) attack from the BM25 side. Per-need `project` routes each query to its corpus. Run: python scripts/eval_retrieval.py --cases scripts/eval_retrieval.volume.cases.json [--lift keywords | --lift-summaries summary]. NOTE: drives the daemon by default — after generating indexes, restart the daemon (or pass --no-daemon) so it serves the rebuilt collections.
